name: DaVinci Resolve MCP
what_it_does: MCP server that bridges AI assistants (Cursor, Claude Desktop) with DaVinci Resolve's scripting API. Enables natural language control of Resolve for project management, timeline operations, media pool management, marker placement, and page/workspace navigation.
use_cases: Querying project and timeline information from DaVinci Resolve via AI chat
Creating new projects and timelines with natural language commands
Importing media files and organizing bins through an AI assistant
Switching between Resolve pages (Edit, Color, Fusion, etc.) via chat
not_for: Complex color grading or Fusion compositing workflows (limited API surface)
Batch rendering or export automation (not documented)
best_when: You use DaVinci Resolve and want to control basic project/timeline/media operations through natural language via Cursor or Claude Desktop.
avoid_when: You need advanced Resolve features (color grading, Fusion, Fairlight), are on Linux, or don't have Resolve 18.5+. The tool surface covers basics, not deep editing.

alternatives.name: DaVinci Resolve Scripting API
alternatives.note: Direct Python scripting without MCP wrapper

alternatives.name: resolve-scripts
alternatives.note: Community Python scripts for Resolve automation

auth.notes: No authentication required. Operates locally, communicating with the local DaVinci Resolve instance.

pricing.notes: MIT licensed. DaVinci Resolve Studio recommended but free version may work for basic features.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: No error handling documentation. Pre-launch verification script helps catch setup issues but runtime errors are undocumented.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Read operations (list, get) are idempotent. Write operations (create project, add marker) are not — duplicates possible.
known_agent_gotchas: DaVinci Resolve must be running before server starts
Environment variables must be set correctly for Resolve scripting API paths
Studio version recommended over free — some API features may be Studio-only
Limited tool surface — covers basics but not advanced color/Fusion/Fairlight
Python path configuration can be tricky on Windows
